steps.title: Calculate the confidence interval
steps.content:
The margin of error defines the range around the sample mean where the true population mean likely falls:
$$8.4 \pm 1.3 = [7.1, \, 9.7]$$
This interval [7.1, 9.7] contains all plausible values for the true population mean.

steps.title: Match to the correct interpretation
steps.content:
Since [7.1, 9.7] is the plausible range for the true mean, any statement claiming a value outside this range is implausible.

Choice A (mean < 7.1) and Choice D (mean > 9.7) both claim values outside the interval.

Choice C confuses the population mean with individual employee values.

The correct answer correctly identifies [7.1, 9.7] as the plausible range for the true mean.

That confirms B is correct.
